question.text: Under SEBI IA Regulations, which professional is authorized to conduct the mandatory annual audit of an investment adviser?

answer.text: Only a Chartered Accountant in practice
Only a Company Secretary in practice
A Chartered Accountant, Company Secretary, or Cost Accountant who is a member of the respective professional institute

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under SEBI IA Regulations, the annual audit of an investment adviser can be conducted by a member of ICAI (Chartered Accountant), ICSI (Company Secretary), or ICWAI (Cost Accountant). The regulation does not restrict the audit to only one category of professional — all three are equally qualified.
**The Trap:** Candidates often assume only CAs can conduct audits. While CAs conduct statutory audits under the Companies Act, the IA annual audit can be performed by CS and Cost Accountant members as well.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Audit requirements for intermediaries vary — compare with MF trustee audits (Series V-A) and PMS compliance audits (Series XII). The IA audit requirement is broader in terms of eligible auditors.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Choose an auditor who understands SEBI IA regulations specifically. A general CA may not be familiar with advisory-specific compliance requirements like suitability documentation or conflict-of-interest disclosures.
